content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Resolved all 3 pending amendments from round-19 review for the success reconciliation handler (bead 9ni.8.5.1). Amendment 1: Added a defensive eprintln warning in try_reconcile_success (daemon_loop.rs:1741-1752) that fires when run_id_from_task (workflow_run_id) is None, alerting operators that the fallback to the latest RunStarted journal event is being used and may pick up the wrong run_id if the project was re-run between failure and retry. This covers tasks dispatched before workflow_run_id was introduced. Amendment 2: Broadened the post-completion retry fast path from reconciliation_*-only to any failure class with a Completed run snapshot. In both daemon_loop.rs paths (multi-repo at line 691 and single-repo at line 1458), replaced the had_reconciliation_failure prefix check with task.failure_class.is_some(), covering pr_runtime_failed and any future post-completion failure class. In claim_task (task_service.rs:158-172), replaced the is_reconciliation_retry prefix check with is_post_completion_retry using FsRunSnapshotStore to read the run snapshot and check RunStatus::Completed, so needs_remote_resume is false for any post-completion retry. Aborted retries (failure_class is None) still start fresh. Amendment 3: Added eager persist_workflow_run_id call at claim time (before dispatch) in both multi-repo (daemon_loop.rs:708) and single-repo (daemon_loop.rs:1470) paths. This closes the crash window between dispatch completion and the post-dispatch persist: for post-completion retries the journal already contains the RunStarted event so the binding is captured; for fresh dispatches this is a no-op (no RunStarted yet) and the post-dispatch call catches those.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added defensive eprintln warning in try_reconcile_success (daemon_loop.rs:1741-1752) when run_id_from_task is None, logging that the fallback to latest RunStarted journal event is being used and may be wrong if the project was re-run between failure and retry. Includes task_id for operator correlation
2. [Completed] Amendment 2 (multi-repo path): Replaced had_reconciliation_failure prefix check with task.failure_class.is_some() at daemon_loop.rs:691. Comment updated to document that the gate covers reconciliation_* failures, pr_runtime_failed, and any future post-completion failure class. Aborted retries (failure_class is None) and fresh dispatches are excluded by the is_some() check
3. [Completed] Amendment 2 (single-repo path): Applied identical broadening at daemon_loop.rs:1458, mirroring the multi-repo fix. Uses the same pre-claim task variable which retains the original failure_class before claim_task's clear_failure()
4. [Completed] Amendment 2 (claim_task): Replaced is_reconciliation_retry prefix check in task_service.rs:158-172 with is_post_completion_retry that reads the run snapshot via FsRunSnapshotStore and checks RunStatus::Completed. needs_remote_resume is now false for any post-completion retry (not just reconciliation_*), preventing try_resume_from_remote from blocking retries that only need post-completion bookkeeping
5. [Completed] Amendment 3 (multi-repo path): Added eager self.persist_workflow_run_id(store_dir, repo_root, &claimed_task) call at daemon_loop.rs:708, before the is_post_completion_retry dispatch gate. Comment documents: closes crash window, for post-completion retries the journal already has RunStarted, for fresh dispatches this is a no-op
6. [Completed] Amendment 3 (single-repo path): Added eager self.persist_workflow_run_id(base_dir, base_dir, &claimed_task) call at daemon_loop.rs:1470, with shorter comment referencing multi-repo rationale
7. [Completed] Ran cargo test (757 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (0 new warnings in modified files, 16 pre-existing in unrelated files), cargo fmt --check (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 757 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (full suite)
- cargo test --lib success_reconciliation: 13 passed (8 unit + 1 e2e + 1 sync_failure_on_replay + 1 terminal_replay_tolerance + 2 hint tests), 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 0 new warnings in modified files (daemon_loop.rs, task_service.rs); 16 pre-existing warnings in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: eprintln warning at daemon_loop.rs:1747-1752 fires when run_id_from_task is None, includes task_id and describes the fallback risk
- Amendment 2 verified: multi-repo is_post_completion_retry at daemon_loop.rs:691 uses task.failure_class.is_some() (not starts_with('reconciliation_')); single-repo at daemon_loop.rs:1458 mirrors; claim_task at task_service.rs:159-172 uses FsRunSnapshotStore + RunStatus::Completed check. All three locations broadened from reconciliation_*-only to any failure_class + Completed snapshot
- Amendment 2 safety verified: aborted retries have failure_class None (set by abort_task at task_service.rs:486-509 which calls clear_failure before setting status to Aborted), so is_some() is false and they take the normal rebase path. Fresh dispatches have failure_class None (initial task creation), so they also take the normal path
- Amendment 3 verified: eager persist_workflow_run_id at daemon_loop.rs:708 (multi-repo) and daemon_loop.rs:1470 (single-repo), both before the is_post_completion_retry dispatch gate. For post-completion retries, the journal RunStarted event from the original dispatch is captured. For fresh dispatches, no RunStarted exists yet so the call is a no-op — the post-dispatch call at lines 877/1541 catches those
- git diff --stat: daemon_loop.rs 69 insertions(+), 38 deletions(-); task_service.rs 37 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
- End-to-end test reconcile_success_end_to_end_updates_milestone continues to pass with all assertions including structural task_id (entry.task_id == Some('task-e2e-123')) and hint persistence (hint file exists with id='bead-next', score=0.8)

## Outstanding Risks

- The lineage task_id extraction still parses the 'task_id=<value>' format from outcome_detail via the extract_task_id_from_outcome_detail helper. This couples the fs adapter to a format defined in success_reconciliation.rs. A future refactor should thread task_id as a first-class parameter through the record_bead_completion/update_task_run chain to eliminate this format dependency.
- The next_step_hint.json file is never deleted by BvFailed — only overwritten by Captured or deleted by NoRecommendation. If bv is permanently unavailable for a milestone, a stale hint from a previous bead may persist indefinitely. This is acceptable since hints are informational and the selection step should treat them as advisory.
- The eager persist_workflow_run_id at claim time is a no-op for fresh dispatches (no RunStarted event yet in the journal). The post-dispatch persist call at lines 877/1541 catches those. For post-completion retries the journal already contains the RunStarted from the original dispatch, so the eager call successfully binds the task to its run before any new work occurs.
- The broadened is_post_completion_retry check uses task.failure_class.is_some() which covers all failure classes, not just post-completion ones. However, the conjunction with RunStatus::Completed from the run snapshot ensures only tasks whose run actually completed are fast-pathed. A task that failed mid-execution would have a non-Completed snapshot and would not match, preserving the normal rebase+dispatch path for genuine execution failures.
